pr.title: Split out item logic tables, match itcapsule
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Split large item logic/data tables out of the monolithic item assembly into a dedicated C translation unit plus a residual asm file, and matched the capsule item logic into src/melee/it/itcapsule.c. The PR moved thousands of bytes of table data out of asm/melee/it/it_266F.s, removed the capsule section from asm/melee/it/it_27CF.s, added itcapsule headers and build objects, and made small item type/header updates needed by capsule state and attributes.
what_changed: Removed the item GX/link and logic table region starting at it_803F1418/it_803F14C4 from asm/melee/it/it_266F.s and recreated the exposed tables in src/melee/it/it_803F1418.c.
Added asm/melee/it/it_803F1418.s as a new residual assembly file for the item table/data region not yet represented in C.
Added src/melee/it/itcapsule.c and src/melee/it/itcapsule.h, replacing the deleted capsule functions from asm/melee/it/it_27CF.s.
Added itcapsule.c.o, it_803F1418.c.o, and it_803F1418.s.o to obj_files.mk near the existing item objects.
Added ItCapsuleAttrs with bool x0 and bool x4 to src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h and added a capsule member to the Item xDD4_itemVar union in src/melee/it/types.h.
Changed ItemCommonData.x68_float from s32 to f32, matching its use as a floating-point value in itcapsule.c.
Added a note in src/melee/it/forward.h that development JP item name strings start at data address 0x803EA7A8.
Removed an extern declaration for it_804D6D28 from src/melee/it/it_266F.h.
smart_moves: Isolated a large static item logic table block into its own translation unit instead of continuing to grow the monolithic it_266F assembly, making future item table edits and per-item header migration easier to search and review.

Preserved object ordering in obj_files.mk by inserting the new it_803F1418 and itcapsule objects between the existing it_266F and it_27CF item objects, which is a common decomp tactic when splitting code/data while keeping addresses stable.

Used C for most capsule behavior but retained inline asm for difficult matching cases: it_8027D338 is guarded by MUST_MATCH because the C version had incorrect stack offsets for an inlined it_8027CF30, and it_8027D530 is also kept as asm.

Factored the repeated capsule collision/opening logic into it_8027D338_autoinlined and reused it from it_8027D414, it_8027D4B8, and it_8027D4F4, while still preserving exact-matching asm where the compiler did not cooperate.
Introduced a small named capsule item-var struct instead of leaving the capsule state as anonymous raw offsets in the Item union.
decomp_lessons: When a data-heavy asm block contains many cross-item function pointers, splitting the tables into a dedicated C file can be a useful intermediate step even before every referenced item has its own complete header.

Temporary extern declarations for table-only function pointers are acceptable when matching large dispatch tables, but reviewers expect them to become proper per-item header declarations as those items are matched.
Prefer explicit empty parameter lists with `(void)` for no-argument C prototypes; bare `()` was called out in review.

If a mostly matched C function differs only because of inlining/stack-offset behavior, keep a readable non-MUST_MATCH C version beside a MUST_MATCH asm implementation to document intent while preserving matching.
Field type corrections can be driven by use sites: x68_float being passed to a function expecting f32 justified changing ItemCommonData.x68_float from s32 to f32.
Adding item-specific state to the Item union under a named member, such as `capsule`, improves reuse for later item decomp work without fully resolving every field name.
naming_conventions: Item module filenames use lowercase item names, e.g. `itcapsule.c` and `itcapsule.h`.
Unresolved functions kept address-style names such as `it_8027CF30`, `it_8027D338`, and `it_803F1418`.
Item-specific attribute structs can use CamelCase with an item prefix, as in `ItCapsuleAttrs`.
Item variable union members can use lowercase item names, as in `it->xDD4_itemVar.capsule.x0` and `.x4`.
Function prototypes for item logic used `Item_GObj*` in the new capsule header and source where the functions operate on item GObjs.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split static data/tables from `asm/melee/it/it_266F.s` into a C table file (`src/melee/it/it_803F1418.c`) plus a new asm remainder (`asm/melee/it/it_803F1418.s`).
Matched capsule logic from `asm/melee/it/it_27CF.s` into C while leaving hard cases as inline asm.
Used `#ifndef MUST_MATCH` / `#else asm` around `it_8027D338` to keep a readable C implementation available when exact matching is not required.
Documented the specific mismatch reason for `it_8027D338`: incorrect stack offsets for an inlined `it_8027CF30`.
Used an `it_8027D338_autoinlined` helper to model repeated compiler-inlined behavior in nearby capsule callbacks.
Preserved function-pointer table structure with `struct sdata_ItemGXLink it_803F1418[43]` and `struct ItemLogicTable it_803F14C4[43]` in C.

review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether the many table function declarations were only used in tables or also called/defined elsewhere, and requested `(void)` instead of `()` for no-argument prototypes.

Explained that the functions are defined in each item's translation unit, take `Item_GObj*`, and that these declarations were temporary until the functions can be moved to individual item headers as those items are matched.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that moved the temporary item function externs from src/melee/it/it_803F1418.c into individual item headers.
Search for subsequent changes to `it_8027D338` or `it_8027D530` to see whether the inline asm was eventually replaced by matching C.
Search later item table splits around `it_803F1418`, `it_803F14C4`, and `ItemLogicTable` for reused table-splitting patterns.
Check later PRs touching `ItCapsuleAttrs` or `xDD4_itemVar.capsule` for field name refinements beyond x0/x4.
